description: Event handler that triggers a skill when the associated entity dies. Used for death-triggered abilities like explosions, spawns, or damage effects.

handler_fields.name: skill
handler_fields.description: Reference to the parent skill that owns this handler. Used to get entity/owner information.

handler_fields.name: effectData
handler_fields.description: Reference to the Deathrattle effect data containing configuration for the death trigger.

effect_fields_from_0x58.name: SkillTemplate
effect_fields_from_0x58.description: Reference to the SkillTemplate that will be instantiated and executed when the entity dies. This defines what skill effect triggers on death (explosion, spawn, buff, etc).

effect_fields_from_0x58.name: TriggerChance
effect_fields_from_0x58.description: Percentage chance (0-100) that the deathrattle will trigger. Defaults to 100 in constructor. Compared against PseudoRandom.NextPercent() roll.

related_skill_fields.name: Position
related_skill_fields.description: Position of the dying entity, copied to triggered skill at offset 0xE8.

related_skill_fields.name: Position
related_skill_fields.description: Target position for skill execution, set to dying entity's position.

related_skill_fields.name: SourceSkill
related_skill_fields.description: Reference to the original skill that contained the deathrattle effect.

actor_fields.name: IsPanicked
actor_fields.description: If non-zero, actor is in panicked state and deathrattle will NOT trigger. This prevents panicked units from executing death effects.

key_discoveries.details: Deathrattle effects are skipped if the dying Actor has a non-zero value at offset 0x16B (panicked state). This is a game balance mechanic preventing panicked units from triggering death effects.
The TriggerChance field at offset 0x60 allows for percentage-based deathrattle activation. Uses PseudoRandom.NextPercent() which generates values 1-100. If roll > TriggerChance, effect is skipped.
The triggered skill inherits the dying entity's position (0x78 -> 0xE8), ensuring effects like explosions occur at the death location.
The triggered skill stores a reference to the original skill (offset 0xF0) maintaining the skill chain for attribution and effects.
The handler resolves the skill owner through the SkillContainer chain (handler.skill -> SkillContainer -> Owner) to properly attribute the death effect.
The skill is executed with trigger type 0x13, which corresponds to the OnDeath event type in the skill system.
Deathrattle.Create() instantiates DeathrattleHandler and stores the effect reference at offset 0x18, following the standard effect->handler creation pattern.
The Deathrattle constructor sets TriggerChance to 100 by default, meaning deathrattles trigger 100% of the time unless explicitly configured otherwise.

functions_analyzed.name: Menace.Tactical.Skills.Effects.DeathrattleHandler$$OnDeath
functions_analyzed.description: Main death event handler - checks panicked state, rolls for trigger chance, creates and executes skill

functions_analyzed.name: Menace.Tactical.Skills.Effects.Deathrattle$$Create
functions_analyzed.description: Factory method - creates DeathrattleHandler instance with effect data reference

comments_added.comment: Deathrattle Effect Data Fields: 0x58 = SkillTemplate, 0x60 = TriggerChance
